headlines.title: 3 killed after car plunges off Highway 9 near Saratoga
headlines.description: Three people were killed after a car crashed off Highway 9 and down a steep embankment near Saratoga in the Santa Cruz Mountains, according to the California Highway Patrol. CHP’s San Jose office said officers responded to a report of a crash on State Route 9 west of Redwood Gulch Road. When officers arrived, they determined the crash involved fatal injuries. Preliminary information indicates a 2024 BMW M3 was traveling eastbound on Highway 9 when the driver, a 21-year-old man from Yuba City, lost control of the vehicle. The car left the roadway and went down a steep embankment. The driver and two passengers were killed in the crash. CHP identified the passengers as a 27-year-old man from Salem, Oregon, and a 17-year-old boy from San Jose. Highway 9 was closed in both directions for about three hours while investigators examined the scene and crews recovered the vehicle. The cause of the crash remains under investigation. CHP said all information is preliminary and subject to change. Anyone who witnessed the crash or has information is asked to contact the CHP San Jose Area Office at 408-961-0900.

headlines.title: World Cup excitement builds ahead of Team USA's Bay Area arrival
headlines.description: The impact of the upcoming USA match is stretching into all parts of the Bay Area. Hotel officials say they have the capacity and are now prepping for July 1 when Team USA makes its debut in the World Cup Round of 32 in Santa Clara. But whether you can get a ticket to the match is a whole other challenge that could be historic. Now that the team has clinched the top spot in its group, San Francisco hotels are making plans. “We talked about it today. I think we’re going to have to step it up for all the Americans that are looking to watch and support their team,” said Peter Hart, general manager of Hilton Union Square and Parc 55. The Hilton Union Square is the biggest hotel on the West Coast, and staff say come one, come all, and book now. “Rates tend to get a little bit higher the closer you get to the event, the more compression that happens. So always good to secure the rooms in advance,” Hart said. “I think rates in the market, in general, are in the $200 to $400 range.” That is chump change compared to what you might be forking out for a single ticket to that Team USA match. A check of StubHub showed tickets ranging from about $2,700 to more than $16,000. Wondering where’s the sweet spot to snag a cheaper ticket? “They should have already had them,” said Dr. Josh Greer, a ticket sales researcher. Greer says the World Cup has become out of reach for the common fan, and the ceiling for those sky-high prices might be record-setting. “This may be the most expensive event if the United States continues to win,” Greer said. “Sports are now a top-level event that most folks want to attend or at least be at, whether it be for status or actually seeing the game, but it’s no longer an event that’s affordable to go to. You’re going to have to save many years in advance,” Greer said. Greer says if you are still planning to go, buy from trusted sites with a buyer guarantee, like StubHub, Ticketmaster or TickPick. And he says don’t forget there are plenty of places to feel part of the magic for free, which is what the city wants visitors to do, and then some. “I would say that if they come here to stretch the trip into a few days because yes, it’s so great to experience the World Cup, it’s a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity, but San Francisco has so much to offer,” said Alex Bastian, CEO of the Hotel Council of San Francisco.

headlines.title: A shooter kills 2 at a Northern California library and an 18-year-old suspect has been arrested
headlines.description: A shooting at a library in Northern California left two people dead and an 18-year-old suspect has been arrested, police said Tuesday. Police responded to a 911 call soon after 5 p.m. Monday. Chico police Chief Billy Aldridge said gunshots and screams could be heard on that call from the Chico branch of the Butte County Library. Chico, a city of about 100,000 people, is 150 miles (240 kilometers) northeast of San Francisco. The suspect fled out the back of the library as officers entered, but additional law enforcement personnel behind the building took the suspect into custody, Aldridge said during a news conference. “The incident this evening was obviously very sad, traumatic for a lot of people. Very traumatic for our community,” Aldridge said. The streets around the library were closed temporarily and a family reunification center was set up for the people who were inside the building. A child was also taken to the hospital with a minor injury. Police later determined the suspect acted alone and identified him as Bradley Scott Sayer of Chico. He was booked into the Butte County Jail on suspicion of two counts of murder. There was no indication he had any prior relationship with or connection to the victims, police said in a statement. Authorities have not released their names. A police department dispatcher early Tuesday did not know if Sayer has a lawyer and no one could be immediately reached at the jail. A search of Butte County court records did not show his name and a phone number could not be found for him. Police said the Butte County Sheriff’s Office and the FBI are assisting in the investigation. All Butte County library branches will be closed Tuesday, officials said. In a Facebook post, the county offered its “deepest condolences to everyone affected, including the victims, their loved ones, library staff, and all those impacted by this heartbreaking incident.” It wasn’t the first act of violence at a U.S. library. A man in Tulsa, Oklahoma, was sentenced to life in prison after pleading guilty to fatally shooting a man in a library and another man in a convenience store in 2023. In 2020, a suspect was sent to a mental health facility after he pleaded guilty to fatally stabbing a library security guard in Spring Valley, New York. A teenager who pleaded guilty to fatally shooting two public library employees in Clovis, New Mexico, in 2017 was also sentenced to life in prison. This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: Emergency crews respond to brush fire in Palo Alto
headlines.description: Emergency responded Monday to a brush fire in Palo Alto. The blaze was reported in the area of Arastradero Road and Caballo Lane, just northwest of Palo Alto Hills Golf & Country Club. Authorities report the fire burned several acres with a slow rate of spread. No other information was immediately available.

headlines.title: San Francisco home with a history of squatters hits the market for $1.3 million
headlines.description: An abandoned house near San Francisco’s Castro neighborhood that has been popular with squatters for years is now for sale. On Yukon Street at the edge of Kite Hill in the Eureka Valley neighborhood, the house with arched windows over the garage, including one that’s broken, is listed for $1.3 million. Listing agent Zara Rowbotham and her brother, James, put together a promotional video highlighting the home’s fixer-upper potential. There is no running water or power at the house. Neighbors have reported to the city that squatters relieve themselves at the top floor atrium. “They needed a place to do it, so they had the nice manners to do it in one basket,” Rowbotham said. “Unfortunately it was an outside basket right in front of one of the neighbors’ houses.” With the nature of San Francisco’s red-hot housing market, Rowbothom said they already have a potential buyer. Rowbothom added the city is swirling with money right now and there are few places to buy, so properties like the one on Yukon Street – even with a history of squatters – are being snapped up quickly. Rowbothom said they’re going for millions of dollars, with people paying cash a lot of the time.

headlines.title: Bay Area braces for highly-anticipated USMNT knockout round match in Santa Clara
headlines.description: Electric. That’s the atmosphere soccer fans are expecting when the United States men’s national team comes to play a knockout round next month in Santa Clara. Tens of thousands of fans have been watching the World Cup matches and rooting on their homes teams on big screen televisions at viewing parties throughout the Bay Area. “The energy is going to be electric no matter who they’re playing,” soccer fan Reno Arzate said of the USMNT playing in the Bay Area. “It’s going to be amazing. I’m looking forward to it.” With the highly-anticipated match inching closer, the resale cost of buying a ticket now ranges around $3,000 or more for the July 1 game. May 13 World Cup Jun 1 2026 FIFA World Cup acronym guide: From FIFA to VAR

headlines.title: World Dog Surfing Championships in Pacifica to celebrate 10-year anniversary
headlines.description: The World Dog Surfing Championships is set to arrive in Pacifica for its 10-year anniversary this summer. Presented by Lagunitas Brewing Company and other premier sponsors, the competition will take place on Linda Mar Beach, also known as Pacifica State Beach. The free events will be held on July 31 and August 1. The main surfing event lasts from 9 a.m. to 2 p.m. on August 1, beginning with dog check ins and ending with an awards ceremony. Animals Mar 31 Animals Aug 2, 2025 Animals Aug 4, 2024 No tickets are required to view the dog surfing competition, but registration is required for dogs participating. While the event is free, there are opportunities to donate to partnered charities or to donate to a team entered in the dog surfing competition. Other events include a pet wellness fair, mobile pet adoptions, costume contests and a “yappy hour” afterwards. To learn more about the breakdown of events, visit surfdogchampionships.com/events.

headlines.title: Bay Area startup opens satellite manufacturing facility in San Jose
headlines.description: A Bay Area space company celebrated the opening of a new facility Monday, bringing both satellites and new jobs to the region. Startup Muon Space officially opened a 130,000-square-foot satellite manufacturing facility in San Jose. The company’s satellites will track everything from weather and national security to Bluetooth devices. On the heels of SpaceX hitting a $2 trillion valuation on IPO day, Muon Space, flush with venture capital funding, has decided to make its move in the industry. NBC Bay Area’s Scott Budman has more in the video player above. SPACEX Jun 13 Elon Musk Jun 12 SPACEX Jun 18 You could already own some SpaceX stocks. Here's how

headlines.title: WalletHub ranks best and worst states for summer road trips
headlines.description: California is known by many for its stunning views, seasonal activities and likeable climate; all features that could add to a positive summer road trip. However, there are many other factors which contribute to the difficulty of traveling within the state. WalletHub ranked California at 47th place in its Best & Worst States for Summer Road Trips (2026) ranking. The state landed in first place for its activities and second for its scenic views, but fell short in other categories. California placed 50th under costs and 43rd under safety, tanking its overall grade. WalletHub broke down cost into subcategories that further explain why California landed toward the bottom: For average gas prices, California placed 50th. For average cost of car repairs, California placed 48th. For price of camping, California placed 48th. California’s total score under WalletHub’s ranking added to 46.56, whereas Minnesota ended up at 61.11, the highest of all 50 states. Review the full report at wallethub.com. Real Estate Jun 18 Bay Area Housing Jun 17 Making It in the Bay Aug 27, 2025 1-bedroom apartment now costs over $3,400 a month to rent in San Francisco

headlines.title: 40 laptops stolen from Antioch nonprofit
headlines.description: An Antioch nonprofit is asking for the public’s help in locating laptops stolen from its learning center. RR Transitional Housing said nearly $20,000 worth of Chromebooks were stolen on Thursday or Friday and a police report has been filed. Sandro Trujillo, who co-founded the program with his wife Marjori Trujillo, said burglars cut camera cables, dismantled the center’s security camera and then pried open its computer lockboxes to steal 40 laptops. “The individuals responsible are attempting to access our bank accounts, PayPal, and Venmo accounts, and we believe they may also be trying to sell the stolen Chromebooks and other devices,” Marjori Trujillo, who also serves as RRTH’s program director, said in an email to NBC Bay Area. The nonprofit provides free tutoring, job readiness and youth development programs. Marjori Trujillo said she urges the community to spread the word as the devices “offer opportunity, hope and education for the children and families they serve.” With just 10 days until the start of their summer program, the RRTH is searching for ways to get the supplies they need. A taco fundraiser will be hosted by RRTH to help replace the stolen laptops. The event will be held 2 p.m. Sunday at the organization’s Antioch center, 512 W. 2nd Street.

headlines.title: Public transportation guide for San Francisco's Pride parade and celebrations
headlines.description: San Francisco’s annual Pride celebration, one of the largest in the world, will kick off during the last weekend of June and transit agencies are ready to help make the experience as smooth as possible. The theme of the 56th annual San Francisco Pride will be “Resistance in Action.” The fair will be located at Civic Center Plaza on Saturday and Sunday, and the parade will march down Market Street on Sunday. The parade will begin at 10:30 a.m. at Embarcadero Plaza and will travel down Market Street before finishing at City Hall. Spectators can watch along the entire 1.6-mile-long route. BART has four stops along the parade route — Embarcadero, Montgomery, Powell and Civic Center stations. San Francisco Jun 21 Giants News Jun 19 San Francisco Jun 5 Saturday will have normal service, but on Sunday the agency plans to help people “ride with pride” by opening at 8 a.m. and offering five-line service. Trains will be scheduled to arrive every five minutes at the downtown stations. After 9 p.m., service will be reduced to three lines. Special event trains will be run between Millbrae and downtown San Francisco, as well as from Pleasant Hill from 9:30 a.m. until 11 a.m., before restarting at 2 p.m. until the evening. Muni will be rerouting due to regular travel down Market Street onto Mission Street. Muni Metro light-rail vehicles will remain in service, allowing attendees to enter and exit at Embarcadero, Montgomery, Powell and Civic Center stations. Caltrain will be offering its half-hourly regular weekend service from 9:30 a.m. to 1 p.m. Riders can then transfer onto a Muni Metro line after arriving in the Downtown San Francisco Fourth and King station. The San Francisco Bay Ferry will be offering additional morning service from Vallejo, Oakland and Alameda alongside their regular weekend service. Riders traveling from Richmond, Alameda or Oakland are offered free parking at ferry-affiliated structures. For those traveling on buses, Alameda-Contra Costa Transit will be offering its regular weekend transbay services to the Salesforce Transit Center. Golden Gate Transit will be operating its buses and ferries on its regular weekend service. Those looking to arrive early are encouraged to take the 9 a.m. Larkspur ferry that will travel directly to the San Francisco Ferry Terminal. More information on the Pride celebration can be found at sfpride.org
headlines.source: Andres Jimenez Larios | Bay City News and Sophia Taylor

headlines.title: Adobe's third semaphore puzzle solved in San Jose after three years
headlines.description: One of the world’s most difficult puzzles created has been solved after three years. Brian Vincent, a San Jose software engineer, was able to crack the code for the third Adobe Semaphore puzzle, the tech giant announced. Vincent solved it this past spring after Adobe launched the creation in May 2023. The first puzzle was displayed in 2006, created by Ben Rubin, an artist who heavily incorporates science and technology into his work. Containing four large LED wheels that rotate and land at different axis positions, the puzzle draws audiences and can take years to solve. The first puzzle was solved in 2007 and the second in 2017. A fourth puzzle is in the making and will be open for anyone to attempt to solve. To hear more about Vincent’s solving strategies and Rubin’s creation of the puzzle, visit adobe.com/sj-semaphore.
